content.problemStatement:
You're given an array AAA of length NNN, with elements between 111 and NNN.

You will perform the following operation **exactly once**:


Choose two indices LLL and RRR (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \leq L \leq R \leq N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N).
Then, choose an integer XXX (1‚â§X‚â§N1 \leq X \leq N1‚â§X‚â§N) such that XXX **does not appear** among [AL,AL+1,AL+2,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L+1}, A_{L+2}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,AL+2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã].
Finally, for each iii from LLL to RRR (inclusive of both), set AiA_iAi‚Äã to XXX.
For example, if A=[3,1,4,1,5]A = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5]A=[3,1,4,1,5], choosing L=2,R=4,X=2L = 2, R = 4, X = 2L=2,R=4,X=2 will result in the array [3,2,2,2,5][3, 2, 2, 2, 5][3,2,2,2,5].

How many of these operations will result in AAA being sorted in non-decreasing order?

Two operations are considered different if the tuples (L,R,X)(L, R, X)(L,R,X) determining them are different.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚Äî the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of operations that result in AAA being sorted in non-descending order.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚ãÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.
